description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring alternating black and white horizontal stripes, reminiscent of a crosswalk or urban barrier. The stripes are evenly spaced and run parallel across the entire top face, creating a visually striking pattern. The sides of the block are solid black, providing a stark contrast to the patterned top. The block appears to be made of a dense, stone-like material, possibly granite or marble, with a polished or semi-polished finish. The overall form is geometric and regular, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. This type of block is commonly used as an obstacle or barrier in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of traffic control installations.
description: A rectangular stone block with a black-and-white striped top and solid black sides, resembling an urban barrier or crosswalk element.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the side, showing horizontal black and white stripes across the top surface and a solid black front face.
description_view_1: Viewed from an angle, the vertical black and white stripes on the top are prominent, with the solid black side visible.
description_view_2: The block is shown from the opposite side, again highlighting the horizontal striped pattern on the top and a solid black face.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the vertical striped pattern on the top surface, with the solid black side in the foreground.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or pedestrian crossing zone
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared flat surface
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned as part of a row of similar blocks
